hard.next_step: 6/17 - No material update - Decision on 6/22 5/13 - Decision Pending. They had PTOs which delayed their process. They will see 2 addtional demos and provide a decision by 6/22 - This is a closed lost who came back to revisit us after seeing the lackluster competition. Now we are up against Zip. 5/4 - Shared Finance Focused Exec summary with Kisco team. Patty will provide an update on their decision Mid May 4/28 - Demo completed, Patty will follow up with next steps by 5/11 4/3 - Demo Scheduled with Patty, CFO (John Hanna) and Chrissie (VP of Accounting) for 4/17 4/2 - Patty (Head of procurement) met with her leadership team and theres renewed interest in Zycus - Moving forward with demo for Kisco Executive team after 4/15
hard.ais_why:
• The 89-minute 12/9/2025 Patty Rice exec demo generated 11 action items. None mention AI, Merlin, Agentic, Anna, or Intake. Ritika Ritolia's commitments cover OCR extraction of late-charge line items from supplier invoices, supplier self-registration via a public web link routed through an approval queue, payment gateway integration, and approve/reject buttons inside email notifications: operational AP and eProcurement plumbing, none AI-led.
• Buyer Patty Rice's single attributed item is receiving a follow-up Excel. Steve Ovadje is committed to a detailed pricing proposal and the follow-up calendar invite. The wider discovery record frames the drivers as PR-to-PO workflow, punch-out support, GL coding automation, invoice routing, and duplicate detection: operationally replacing the sunsetting Procurement Partners system.
• AI is flagged Very Critical at the account level, but no buyer-stated AI requirement, AI demo ask, or AI POC has surfaced across seven opportunity-level Avoma calls. Transcript keyword review confirmed AI signal absent in buyer attribution. Until the account-level AI intent shows up inside this deal, the score holds in AI Resistant and confidence moves to High.

north_star_verdict.headline: Kisco told Steve in writing on 4 Jun it is focusing on Coupa with Zycus a fallback; the committed 22 Jun decision passed with no update and Patty was out of office through 6 Jul, so momentum has thinned on a price-sensitive final-two evaluation.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 7 Nov 2025, ~8 months in cycle and ~3.7 months in Shortlisted (qualified 16 Mar 2026). Close date pushed four times since April (latest 30 Jun by Michael McCarthy: 23 Jul -> 12 Aug), leaving 37 days to close. The buyer-committed 22 Jun decision passed with 'no material update' (Next Step 17 Jun); last genuine two-way buyer touch was Patty Rice's 4 Jun email naming Coupa as the current focus, with only rep outreach since (29 Jun, auto-reply OOO to 6 Jul). No commercials accepted, no MSA, EB not bought in. The real forcing event is the Procurement Partners sunset / forced Oracle On-Care migration in Sept-Oct 2026, not 12 Aug.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (downgrade from Upside Key Deal): buyer has stated a competitor preference in writing, no budget approved, no commercials accepted, decision date has slipped repeatedly.
evidence: Patty Rice, 4 Jun 2026 email: 'Candidly, we are focusing on the proficiencies of the Coupa platform right now, but if we run into any non-starters, we will need to switch gears.'
Next Step 17 Jun 2026: 'No material update - Decision on 6/22' (the 22 Jun date has since passed).
Next Step 13 May 2026: 'This is a closed lost who came back to revisit us after seeing the lackluster competition. Now we are up against Zip.'
Patty Rice auto-reply 29 Jun 2026: out of office 6/29/26 to 7/6/26.
CloseDate 30 Jun 2026: 23 Jul -> 12 Aug (by Michael McCarthy), a manager-driven slip.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is well defined but only operationally quantified. On the 17 Apr 2026 follow-up demo the Zycus SC framed Kisco's state as spend 'largely uncontrolled,' purchases happening outside the system, and only 80% of actual spend captured across communities, with a lean AP team of three clearing invoice exceptions in 24-48 hours (confirmed by the buyer AP speaker on that call). Prior discovery put incumbent Procurement Partners spend near $38k/year across ~32 communities. What is missing is a Zycus-specific ROI/savings case: no dollar value of avoided duplicate payments, recovered rebates, or cycle-time savings has been put to finance, which is exactly why the CFO has not committed budget. Quantifying that value for John Hanna is the ranked move.
sources: Avoma follow-up demo 17 Apr 2026 (Zycus SC + Kisco AP speaker)
Avoma catch-up 11 Feb 2026 (Patty Rice, rebate/spend detail)

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is John Hanna, Kisco's CFO. Patty Rice named him on the 17 Apr 2026 demo ('John Hannah is our CFO... he's over our IT team primarily') and on 9 Dec 2025 said the invoicing replacement needs her VP of Finance to see it because 'I'm not gonna be the ultimate one to decide that.' Hanna attended the 17 Apr exec demo but dropped off before the close and made no budget commitment, and Chrissie Ripa (VP Finance/Accounting) co-owns the AP/invoicing half of the decision. So the EB is mapped but not engaged or bought in: Patty controls only the front-end purchasing choice. The move is a finance-owned ROI conversation directly with Hanna and Ripa, not another Patty-level demo.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are clear from the calls: (1) AI invoice-reading accuracy, where Patty rated Automate/Ottimate's '100% AI' above OCR (11 Feb 2026) and doubted Coupa's 'backend invoicing piece' (17 Apr); (2) an integrated single-platform P2P vs a split stack; (3) NetSuite integration and GL-coding flexibility, probed hard by John Hanna and Patty on 9 Dec and 17 Apr; (4) punch-out coverage (Grainger, HD Supply, Amazon Business, Clark National Accounts, ~24 punch-outs) and EDI ingestion; (5) price close to current spend; and (6) contract term, with Kisco resisting the 5-year minimum. There is no formal RFP: Patty said on 9 Dec 'I'm not sending out an RFP email... at some point I expect you're gonna present a proposal.' Evaluation is proposal/functionality comparison across the final two to three.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence: Patty runs the front-end vetting, narrows to a final two to three, then convenes finance and IT. The 17 Apr exec demo was the escalation point where she brought in John Hanna, VP Finance Chrissie Ripa and (invited) VP IT Justin Witkowski, then went to internal debrief. She told Steve she needed 'two to three weeks' before circling back and committed a decision by 22 Jun, which passed with no update. The process is real but slipping: the decision target moved from end-2025 to April to 22 Jun and the close has been pushed four times. No formal RFP, proposal-based comparison, POC only offered after down-selection. The gap is a co-authored, dated decision plan the buyer owns.

paper_process.narrative: The deal has not reached contracting: no MSA, order form, or redlines are in play, and pricing has been shared but not accepted. Two paper frictions are already visible from the 11 Feb pricing call: Kisco 'does not like 5 year agreements' and asked about a 3-year option (Zycus's discount is tied to the 5-year term), and Kisco pays annually only. A POC will only be offered after Zycus is down-selected as vendor (stated 9 Dec). The forcing function is external, not contractual: Procurement Partners is force-migrating customers to the relevant stakeholder in Sept-Oct 2026, which Patty rejects, so a replacement decision is mandatory before then. Visibility improves once the buyer commits to a decision date and term structure.
sources: Avoma demo pt2 9 Dec 2025 (POC after down-select)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is concrete and buyer-articulated: spend is uncontrolled and fragmented across ~32 senior-living communities, only 80% of spend is captured, invoices bypass procurement into the incumbent AP system (Avid Exchange), duplicate payments slip through, and a three-person AP team spends most of its time clearing exception queues in 24-48 hours. Punch-out and EDI usage is scattered across 24+ vendors with per-community credit-application and banking complexity. The trigger pain is that incumbent Procurement Partners is being sunset and force-migrated to the relevant stakeholder, which Patty explicitly dislikes, so status quo is not an option. Pain owners: Patty Rice (procurement/front-end) and the AP/finance team under Chrissie Ripa and John Hanna (invoicing).
sources: Avoma follow-up demo 17 Apr 2026 (Kisco AP speaker + Patty Rice)

champion.narrative: Patty Rice (Purchasing Analyst, effectively head of procurement) is a developing but at-risk champion. She single-handedly revived this closed-lost deal ('what I was asked for was to revisit my top 3... Zycus is one of them,' 17 Apr) and pulled her CFO, VP Finance and VP IT into the exec demo, and says she wants to 'stay on track.' What is still missing is decisive advocacy for Zycus specifically: she owns only the front-end purchasing choice, not budget or the invoicing decision, and on 4 Jun she told the relevant stakeholder is 'focusing on the proficiencies of the Coupa platform right now.' She is our access but is currently leaning to a rival, so she is an advocate we do not yet control. Securing her (and building a second thread to finance) ranks ahead of any commercial step.

competition.narrative: This is a live multi-vendor evaluation and Zycus is not the current leader. Coupa is the buyer's stated current focus (Patty, 4 Jun), making it the strongest present threat despite our ex-Coupa reps (Steve Ovadje, VP Sales Michael McCarthy) knowing its eInvoicing/OCR gaps. The cheaper split-stack SmartPO/SmartProcure (front-end) + Automate/Ottimate (AI invoicing) is the value threat Patty was leaning toward on 11 Feb ('pricing is a lot closer to what we're paying today,' Automate is '100% AI'). Zip is a newer intake entrant she named on 17 Apr and 13 May. GEP was dropped (11 Feb). Incumbent Procurement Partners is being displaced by its forced Oracle On-Care migration. The wedge: single-code-line integrated suite vs Coupa's acquired stack, AP Smartdesk AI parity vs Automate proven to the AP team, and Merlin Intake-in-Teams for non-desk facility staff a purchasing-only tool cannot match.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum has thinned. After the 17 Apr exec demo the buyer went to internal debrief and largely quiet; the committed 22 Jun decision passed with 'no material update,' the last genuine two-way was Patty's 4 Jun email naming Coupa, and the only later activity is rep outreach (29 Jun) plus Patty's OOO auto-reply through 6 Jul. The close date has been pushed four times, most recently by Michael McCarthy on 30 Jun to 12 Aug.

items.change: Manager Michael McCarthy pushed close date 23 Jul -> 12 Aug (fourth slip since April)
Steve emailed Patty seeking to confirm the Coupa lean; received OOO auto-reply (out through 6 Jul)
Committed 22 Jun decision passed with 'no material update' logged 17 Jun
Patty stated in writing Kisco is focusing on Coupa, Zycus a fallback
Next Step: deal is a closed-lost that came back; now up against Zip
Executive demo held with CFO John Hanna and VP Finance Chrissie Ripa; John dropped off early

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is behind on the current internal lean. Coupa is the single strongest current threat: Patty stated 4 Jun 2026 Kisco is 'focusing on the proficiencies of the Coupa platform right now,' with Zycus a fallback. The SmartPO/SmartProcure + Automate/Ottimate split-stack is the live lower-cost threat (Patty leaning to it 11 Feb, rated Automate's AI invoicing above Zycus's). Zip is a newer intake entrant (17 Apr / 13 May). GEP was dropped (11 Feb). Incumbent Procurement Partners is being displaced by its forced Oracle On-Care migration (Sept-Oct 2026).

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Candidly, we are focusing on the proficiencies of the Coupa platform right now, but if we run into any non-starters, we will need to switch gears.
competitors.how_we_win: Force a finance-owned ROI comparison to John Hanna and Chrissie Ripa; differentiate on single-code-line suite vs Coupa's acquired stack and the eInvoicing/OCR limitations Patty herself flagged; leverage ex-Coupa reps' first-hand knowledge of its backend gaps.

competitors.name: SmartPO / SmartProcure + Automate (Ottimate)
competitors.quote: the pricing is a lot closer to what we're paying today... automate is a 100% AI... it reads with a much higher level of accuracy than OCR.
competitors.source: Avoma catch-up 11 Feb 2026 and follow-up demo 17 Apr 2026 (Patty Rice)
competitors.how_we_win: Reframe on total cost and integration risk of stitching two vendors vs one AI-native suite; demo AP Smartdesk AI extraction to the 3-person AP team to neutralise the '100% AI' perception; show Merlin Intake-in-Teams for non-desk facility staff a bolt-on cannot deliver.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: Now we are up against Zip.
competitors.source: Next Step 13 May 2026; Patty named 'Zyp' on 17 Apr call
competitors.how_we_win: Position full source-to-pay depth plus Merlin Intake-in-Teams against Zip's narrower intake-orchestration footprint.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: I have dropped GEP out of the content, so really focusing now on the last two I mentioned.
competitors.how_we_win: No action; eliminated by the buyer.

competitors.name: Procurement Partners (incumbent, forced to Oracle On-Care)
competitors.quote: they are gonna be sunsetting the platform... force migrate all of their existing customers over to On-Care... I don't care for it.
competitors.how_we_win: Use the Sept-Oct 2026 forced-migration deadline as the compelling event: manual is not an option and On-Care is rejected, so a replacement decision is mandatory before then.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Kisco wants one integrated platform but is price-sensitive and skeptical on AI invoicing accuracy and term length. We are aligned on integrated P2P and the compelling event, exposed on price, AI-invoicing proof, and the 5-year term.

items.quote: it doesn't make sense nowadays to have two separate functions for this process. Having it all in one does make most sense.
automate is a 100% AI. So it reads with a much higher level of accuracy than OCR.
the pricing is a lot closer to what we're paying today
we perform a real time integration with your ERP system... we can mirror that into the system.
I just know Kisco does not like 5 year agreements.

items.requirement: Confirm timeframe and cost to enable the Clark National Accounts punch-out
items.quote: if let's say, for example, you don't have Clark Pro... set up, but we needed them as part of this, what would the time frame be?

items.deliverable: Send Patty the list of active/ready punch-out vendors, confirming Clark National Accounts
items.grounding_quote: I can go get the list of active ones that we're already ready to go with. I think that could help too.
items.source: Avoma follow-up demo 17 Apr 2026 (Zycus SC, speaker 6)

items.deliverable: Provide the internal-debrief decision / where Zycus stands after the exec demo

items.quote: I think I have to debrief with John to speak to that one... he might be looking for different things than I am

items.area: No quantified Zycus ROI/savings case for finance
items.quote: I do need to understand what our pricing is... pricing will be a factor for sure.

items.area: No formal decision date since 22 Jun slipped

best_practice_check.summary: Zycus is a price-challenged fallback behind Coupa in a final-two evaluation with a split-ownership buying committee. The win levers are a finance-owned ROI case, an AP-team AI-invoicing proof, and a co-authored decision timeline pinned to the forced-migration event.
flags: Break the Coupa lean with a finance-owned ROI case to John Hanna and VP Finance Chrissie Ripa, not another Patty-level demo.
Neutralise the Automate '100% AI' perception by demoing AP Smartdesk AI extraction directly to Kisco's 3-person AP team.
Co-author a dated decision plan with Patty tied to the Sept-Oct Procurement Partners forced-migration deadline to stop the repeated slips.

items.name: Patty Rice
items.title: Purchasing Analyst
items.risk: Owns only front-end purchasing, not budget or invoicing; at-risk advocate now leaning to a rival

champion_strength.summary: Patty Rice revived this closed-lost deal and pulled in her CFO, VP Finance and VP IT for the 17 Apr exec demo, but she controls only the front-end purchasing decision, not budget or invoicing, and on 4 Jun signalled she is leaning toward Coupa. She is our access but is not decisively advocating for Zycus.
champion_strength.source: Avoma follow-up demo 17 Apr 2026; Email 4 Jun 2026 (Patty Rice)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is 3.0 Negative (AI Resistant): AI is flagged Very Critical at the account level, but no buyer-stated AI requirement, demo ask, or POC surfaced across the calls. Merlin Intake and AP Smartdesk AI were shown, yet the buyer engaged on operational plumbing (punch-outs, GL coding, routing, duplicate detection), not the agentic story. The Merlin/AI narrative is under-landed and, ironically, the competitor differentiator (Automate '100% AI') is AI-led while ours is not registering.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c 3.0, AIS_Status__c Negative. Calls confirm the field: buyer attribution shows operational AP/eProc needs, no AI-led requirement. The one AI-adjacent lever is AP Smartdesk accuracy vs Automate, which is a competitive-parity proof, not a demonstrated AI appetite.

items.detail: Coupa is the buyer's stated current focus; Zycus is an explicit fallback ('if we run into non-starters, we will need to switch gears')
$135k sits well above the cheaper SmartPO+Automate split (~$38k incumbent spend); no finance-owned ROI case landed and CFO has not committed budget
items.source: Avoma catch-up 11 Feb 2026; follow-up demo 17 Apr 2026

items.detail: Economic buyer John Hanna attended one demo and dropped off early; invoicing decision owned by finance/AP, purchasing by Patty (split ownership, no single sponsor)
Decision date slipped from end-2025 to April to 22 Jun (passed); close pushed four times, latest to 12 Aug
Kisco resists Zycus's 5-year minimum term ('Kisco does not like 5 year agreements') where the discount is tied to the 5-year commitment

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: no buyer call since 17 Apr (~80 days), the committed 22 Jun decision passed silently, the last two-way was the 4 Jun Coupa-lean email, and only rep outreach has followed. Zycus stays alive as a final-two fallback with a real compelling event, but is not the current leader.
items: Last genuine two-way buyer touch: 4 Jun 2026 email (Coupa focus).
No Avoma buyer call since 17 Apr 2026.
Committed 22 Jun decision passed with no material update.
Patty out of office 29 Jun - 6 Jul 2026.

customer_preference.evidence: Patty Rice, 4 Jun 2026: 'we are focusing on the proficiencies of the Coupa platform right now'; Zycus positioned as a fallback only.

business_case.evidence: Operational pain quantified (80% spend visibility, ~$38k incumbent spend, 3-person AP team, 24-48h exception handling) but no Zycus ROI/savings figure articulated or accepted by finance.

scope_change.from: Full P2P suite: iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake
scope_change.to: Same P2P suite; buyer is weighing a split purchasing+invoicing stack from rivals, not narrowing Zycus's own scope
scope_change.detail: No reduction in Zycus scope evidenced; the split-solution risk is competitive (SmartPO + Automate), not a Zycus module cut.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We're a price-challenged final-two fallback behind Coupa on a committee we only half-own; the one thing between us and a real shot is a finance-owned ROI case to CFO John Hanna that reverses the Coupa lean before the Sept-Oct forced-migration decision.

win_position.text: Win holds in the high-40s and is capped below 50: Zycus is a named finalist in Kisco's top two-to-three with the forced Procurement Partners-to-Oracle On-Care migration (Sept-Oct 2026) as a genuine compelling event, but Patty told Steve in writing on 4 Jun that Kisco is 'focusing on the proficiencies of the Coupa platform right now,' so we cannot rate higher until that lean reverses.

Price is the exposed flank: at $135k Zycus sits well above the SmartPO + Automate/Ottimate split Patty called 'a lot closer to what we're paying today' (~$38k incumbent), and no finance-owned ROI case has reached CFO John Hanna.

The wedge is real: ex-Coupa reps Steve Ovadje and VP Sales Michael McCarthy know Coupa's eInvoicing/OCR gaps first-hand and Patty herself doubted Coupa's 'backend invoicing piece' on 17 Apr, giving Zycus an opening on the AP half against both Coupa and Automate.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has stalled: the buyer-committed 22 Jun decision passed with 'no material update' and the last genuine two-way was Patty's 4 Jun email, with only unanswered rep outreach since (29 Jun, Patty OOO through 6 Jul).
The close date has been pushed four times since April (latest 30 Jun by Michael McCarthy, 23 Jul to 12 Aug), a manager-driven slip rather than a buyer-set date.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is soft and split: Patty owns only the front-end purchasing choice, while the invoicing decision needs AP-team and VP Finance Chrissie Ripa buy-in and CFO John Hanna has committed no budget.
Kisco resists Zycus's 5-year minimum term ('Kisco does not like 5 year agreements,' 11 Feb) and pays annually only, signalling commercial friction before any signature.

deal_risk.text: Primary risk is a competitive loss to Coupa, the buyer's stated current focus, plus the cheaper SmartPO+Automate split for the AP-owned invoicing half.
Secondary risk is do-nothing slippage past the Sept-Oct forced-migration window if no buyer-owned decision timeline is co-authored.

deal_momentum.evidence: Committed 22 Jun decision passed silently; last two-way 4 Jun; four close-date slips.

customer_commitment.evidence: Split committee ownership, no budget commitment, 5-year-term resistance.

deal_risk.evidence: Buyer leaning Coupa in writing; cheaper split-stack alternative; repeated slips.

expansion_context.note: Net-new deal (a prior closed-lost that the buyer revived), not an expansion into a won account; executive access exists via the 17 Apr exec demo but no prior Zycus win to inherit.

items.action: Re-engage Patty now she is back and reconfirm where Zycus stands versus Coupa.
items.trigger: Patty OOO through 6 Jul; her 4 Jun email named Coupa as the current focus and the 22 Jun decision passed silently.
items.expected_effect: Restarts the stalled thread and surfaces whether Zycus is still live before the Coupa lean hardens.

items.action: Book a finance ROI review with John Hanna and VP Finance Chrissie Ripa.
items.trigger: CFO attended one demo and dropped off; no ROI case has reached finance and budget is uncommitted.
items.expected_effect: Moves the decision to the economic buyer and justifies the premium over Coupa and the SmartPO+Automate split.

items.action: Run an AP Smartdesk AI-extraction demo for Kisco's three-person AP team.
items.trigger: Patty rated Automate/Ottimate's '100% AI' invoice reading above OCR and doubted Coupa's backend invoicing.
items.expected_effect: Neutralises the competitor AI-accuracy claim and wins the AP-owned invoicing half of the decision.

items.action: Co-author a dated decision plan with Patty pinned to the Sept-Oct forced-migration deadline.
items.trigger: Procurement Partners is force-migrating to the relevant stakeholder 2026, which Patty rejects; decision date has slipped four times.
items.expected_effect: Converts the compelling event into a buyer-owned timeline and stops the repeated close-date slips.

items.action: Multi-thread to VP IT Justin Witkowski to validate the NetSuite integration.
items.trigger: Justin joined the 17 Apr demo late and never replied to the 29 Apr follow-up; he owns integration sign-off.
items.expected_effect: Removes a silent gatekeeper and de-risks the ERP integration ahead of any decision.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 10 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.
Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 41 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 49 vs stage-expected 56.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 49 (roughly flat): competitor praised (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 16 is anchored on the stage win (41), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 49, and dragged by risk 32.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 5 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, competitive, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: Kisco Senior Living — Shortlisted · $135K · closes 12 Aug
cro_panel.intro: We're a price-challenged final-two fallback behind Coupa on a committee we only half-own; the one thing between us and a real shot is a finance-owned ROI case to CFO John Hanna that reverses the Coupa lean before the Sept-Oct forced-migration decision.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Win holds in the high-40s and is capped below 50: Zycus is a named finalist in Kisco's top two-to-three with the forced Procurement Partners-to-Oracle On-Care migration (Sept-Oct 2026) as a genuine compelling event, but Patty told Steve in writing on 4 Jun that Kisco is 'focusing on the proficiencies of the Coupa platform right now,' so we cannot rate higher until that lean reverses.

Price is the exposed flank: at $135k Zycus sits well above the SmartPO + Automate/Ottimate split Patty called 'a lot closer to what we're paying today' (~$38k incumbent), and no finance-owned ROI case has reached CFO John Hanna.

The wedge is real: ex-Coupa reps Steve Ovadje and VP Sales Michael McCarthy know Coupa's eInvoicing/OCR gaps first-hand and Patty herself doubted Coupa's 'backend invoicing piece' on 17 Apr, giving Zycus an opening on the AP half against both Coupa and Automate.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 41.

bullets.text: Momentum has stalled: the buyer-committed 22 Jun decision passed with 'no material update' and the last genuine two-way was Patty's 4 Jun email, with only unanswered rep outreach since (29 Jun, Patty OOO through 6 Jul).
The close date has been pushed four times since April (latest 30 Jun by Michael McCarthy, 23 Jul to 12 Aug), a manager-driven slip rather than a buyer-set date.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.

bullets.text: Primary risk is a competitive loss to Coupa, the buyer's stated current focus, plus the cheaper SmartPO+Automate split for the AP-owned invoicing half.
Secondary risk is do-nothing slippage past the Sept-Oct forced-migration window if no buyer-owned decision timeline is co-authored.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Zycus is behind on the current internal lean.

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: Commitment is soft and split: Patty owns only the front-end purchasing choice, while the invoicing decision needs AP-team and VP Finance Chrissie Ripa buy-in and CFO John Hanna has committed no budget.
Kisco resists Zycus's 5-year minimum term ('Kisco does not like 5 year agreements,' 11 Feb) and pays annually only, signalling commercial friction before any signature.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Re-engage Patty now she is back and reconfirm where Zycus stands versus Coupa. (by 10 Jul)
Book a finance ROI review with John Hanna and VP Finance Chrissie Ripa. (by 17 Jul)
Run an AP Smartdesk AI-extraction demo for Kisco's three-person AP team. (by 20 Jul)

Economic buyer John Hanna has never engaged beyond one partial demo; budget authority confirmed by role, not by a commitment.
No Zycus ROI/savings figure has been quantified for or accepted by finance.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Oracle On-Care Sept-Oct')
